name: Tafsir al-Mizan
author: Allamah Muhammad Husayn Tabatabai
text:
# Ayahs

```arabic
مِّمَّا خَطِيٓـَٔـٰتِهِمْ أُغْرِقُوا۟ فَأُدْخِلُوا۟ نَارًا فَلَمْ يَجِدُوا۟ لَهُم مِّن دُونِ ٱللَّهِ أَنصَارًا (٢٥)
```

```english
They were drowned because of their iniquities, then made to enter a Fire, and they did not find any helpers for themselves besides Allah. (25)
```

```arabic
وَقَالَ نُوحٌ رَّبِّ لَا تَذَرْ عَلَى ٱلْأَرْضِ مِنَ ٱلْكَـٰفِرِينَ دَيَّارًا (٢٦)
```

```english
And Noah said, ‘My Lord! ‘Do not leave on the earth any inhabitant from among the faithless. (26)
```

```arabic
إِنَّكَ إِن تَذَرْهُمْ يُضِلُّوا۟ عِبَادَكَ وَلَا يَلِدُوٓا۟ إِلَّا فَاجِرًا كَفَّارًا (٢٧)
```

```english
If You leave them, they will lead astray Your servants, and will beget none except vicious ingrates. (27)
```

```arabic
رَّبِّ ٱغْفِرْ لِى وَلِوَٰلِدَىَّ وَلِمَن دَخَلَ بَيْتِىَ مُؤْمِنًا وَلِلْمُؤْمِنِينَ وَٱلْمُؤْمِنَـٰتِ وَلَا تَزِدِ ٱلظَّـٰلِمِينَ إِلَّا تَبَارًۢا (٢٨)
```

```english
My Lord! Forgive me and my parents, and whoever enters my house in faith, and the faithful men and women, and do not increase the wrongdoers in anything but ruin.’ (28)
```

## (Commentary)

The verses encompass the destruction of the people and the conclusion of the supplication of Nuh (peace be upon him) against them.

**His saying, the Exalted:** **مِمَّا خَطِيئَاتِهِمْ أُغْرِقُوا فَأُدْخِلُوا نَارًا** — **"Because of their sins they were drowned and then made to enter a Fire"** (71:25). The particle "from" (مِنْ) denotes the starting point of an end (ابتداء الغاية), which, depending on the context, signifies causality (التعليل). The "what" (مَا) in "from what" (مِمَّا) is extra (زائدة) to emphasize and magnify the gravity of the sins. Sins (الخطيئات) refer to acts of disobedience and transgressions, and the use of the indefinite form for "Fire" (ناراً) is for magnification (التفخيم) — *the rhetorical device used to emphasize the greatness or intensity of a thing*.

The meaning is: because of their acts of disobedience and their sins, they were drowned by the Flood, and then they were entered — Allah entered them — into a Fire whose punishment cannot be measured by any measure. Among the subtle aspects of the verse's composition is the juxtaposition of drowning by water and being entered into Fire.

What is meant by the Fire is the fire of the intermediate realm (نار البرزخ) — *the state between death and resurrection* — by which the criminals are punished between death and the Resurrection, rather than the Fire of the Hereafter. This verse is among the proofs for the intermediate realm (البرزخ), as the meaning is not that they were drowned and will enter the Fire on the Day of Resurrection. No heed should be paid to the claim that it is permissible for it to refer to the Fire of the Hereafter.

His saying: **فَلَمْ يَجِدُوا لَهُمْ مِنْ دُونِ اللَّهِ أَنْصَارًا** — **"and they did not find any helpers for themselves besides Allah"** (71:25), meaning helpers who could assist them in averting destruction and punishment from them. This is an allusion (تعريض) to their idols and false deities.

**His saying, the Exalted:** **وَقَالَ نُوحٌ رَبِّ لَا تَذَرْ عَلَى الْأَرْضِ مِنَ الْكَافِرِينَ دَيَّارًا** — **"And Noah said, ‘My Lord! Do not leave on the earth any inhabitant from among the faithless"** (71:26). An inhabitant (الديار) is one who dwells in a house (نازل الدار). This verse is the completion of his (peace be upon him) supplication against them. His saying: **مِمَّا خَطِيئَاتِهِمْ أُغْرِقُوا** — **"Because of their sins they were drowned"** (71:25) etc., was a parenthetical statement (معترض) occurring between the two segments of the supplication to indicate that they were destroyed because of the sins Nuh had enumerated, and to serve as a preamble to his request for their destruction, thereby clarifying that their drowning was an answer to his prayer and that the punishment encompassed them to the very last man.

**His saying, the Exalted:** **إِنَّكَ إِنْ تَذَرْهُمْ يُضِلُّوا عِبَادَكَ وَلَا يَلِدُوا إِلَّا فَاجِرًا كَفَّارًا** — **"If You leave them, they will lead Your servants astray, and they will beget none but vicious ingrates"** (71:27). This is the justification (تعليل) for the request to destroy them to the last man. Its import is that there is no benefit in their remaining: neither for the believers besides them, for they would lead them astray; nor in the children they would beget, for they would beget none but vicious ingrates. Viciousness (الفجور) is heinous transgression (الفسق الشنيع), and an ingrate (الكفار) is one who is extreme in disbelief (الكفر).

He (peace be upon him) derived what he mentioned regarding their attributes from divine revelation (الوحي الإلهي), as previously discussed in the commentary on the story of Nuh in Surah Hud.

**His saying, the Exalted:** **رَبِّ اغْفِرْ لِي وَلِوَالِدَيَّ وَلِمَنْ دَخَلَ بَيْتِيَ مُؤْمِنًا وَلِلْمُؤْمِنِينَ وَالْمُؤْمِنَاتِ** — **"My Lord! Forgive me and my parents, and whoever enters my house being a believer, and the faithful men and women"** (71:28).

What is meant by "whoever enters my house being a believer" are those of his people who believed in him, and by "the faithful men and women" is meant the generality of them until the Day of Resurrection.

His saying: **وَلَا تَزِدِ الظَّالِمِينَ إِلَّا تَبَارًا** — **"and do not increase the wrongdoers in anything but ruin’"** (71:28). Ruin (التبار) is destruction (الهلاك). It appears that what is meant by ruin is that which necessitates the punishment of the Hereafter — which is misguidance (الضلال) — and the destruction of this world by drowning. Both have already been mentioned in his supplication. This supplication is the last of what has been narrated of his (peace be upon him) speech in the Noble Quran.
